revision.text:
[[ja:穂積氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Hozumi Clan | image = | surname = Hozumi {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|qid=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}
later Hozumi {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|qid=Q4807592|lt=Ason|1=Ason}} | founder = {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | origin = | ancestral_ancestor = {{ill|Oomizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰|lt=Oomizuguchi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Shinbu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Tenjin and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|lt=Tenjin and Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin and Kunitsukami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|Yamabe District, Nara|en|Yamabe District, Nara|qid=Q1130739|lt=Yamanobe District|1=Yamabe District, Nara}} Hozumi-mura?
{{ill|Toichi district|ja|十市郡|lt=Toichi district|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toichi district|qq=}} Hotsu-mura? | notable_persons = ''' {{ill|Hozumi Oshiyama|ja|穂積押山|lt=Hozumi Oshiyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Oshiyama|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Hozumi Soso|ja|穂積祖足|lt=Hozumi Soso|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Soso|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Hozumi no Kujii|lt=Hozumi no Kujii|lt=Hozumi So|ja|穂積咋|lt=Hozumi no Kujii|lt=Hozumi So|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi So|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Hozumi no Momotari|lt=Hozumi no Momotari|lt=Hozumi Momotatsu|ja|穂積百足|lt=Hozumi no Momotari|lt=Hozumi Momotatsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Momotatsu|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Hozumi Mushimaro|ja|穂積虫麻呂|lt=Hozumi Mushimaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Mushimaro|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Hozumi Oshii|ja|穂積老|lt=Hozumi Oshii|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Oshii|1=Hozumi no Oyu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22129332}} | descendants = ''' {{ill|Kizumi Clan|ja|木積氏|lt=Kizumi Clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizumi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shashke|1=Shake (social class)}}) ''' {{ill|Fujishiro Suzuki Clan|ja|藤白鈴木氏|lt=Fujishiro Suzuki Clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Suzuki Clan|1=Fujishiro Suzuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18224450}} ({{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shashke|1=Shake (social class)}} & {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) ''' {{ill|Hozumi Family (Iyo Province)|ja|穂積家 (伊予国)|lt=Hozumi Family|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Family (Iyo Province)|1=Hozumi Family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20038299}} ({{ill|Buke|ja|武家|lt=Buke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} & {{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|qid=Q241734|lt=Kazoku|1=Kazoku}} ({{ill|Baron|en|Baron|qid=Q165503|lt=Baron|1=Baron}})) | shrine = {{Plainlist| ** {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qid=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha|1=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} ** {{ill|Monobe Jinja|ja|物部神社|lt=Monobe Jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monobe Jinja|1=Mononobe Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11570698}} etc. }} }} '''Hozumi Clan''' (ほづみうじ/ほつみうじ) is a {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|qid=Q2575910|lt=clan|1=Uji (clan)}} whose name is "Hozumi." Their {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|lt=Kabane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} originally were {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|qid=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}, and later changed their {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|qid=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}} surname in the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|lt=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} (684) with the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}. {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|Yamabe District, Nara|en|Yamabe District, Nara|qid=Q1130739|lt=Yamanobe District|1=Yamabe District, Nara}} Hozumi-mura and {{ill|Toichi district|ja|十市郡|lt=Toichi district|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toichi district|qq=}} Hotsu-mura were the strongholds of this {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|qid=Q41493|lt=ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|qid=Q84066|lt=gōzoku|1=Gōzoku}} clan. They are a {{ill|Shinbu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} clan descended from {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}[https://www.lunmu.io/hozumi/ The ancient origins of the Hozumi clan], who entered Yamato before {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, and are related to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Monobe Clan|1=Mononobe clan}}, {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|lt=Uneme Clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumano Kokuzō|ja|熊野国造|lt=Kumano Kokuzō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kokuzō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sueha Kokuzō|ja|末羅国造|lt=Sueha Kokuzō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueha Kokuzō|qq=}} clans. == Origin == In the '''Shinsen Sei Shiryo Roku''' (『新撰姓氏録』), {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} is described as the sixth-generation grandson of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Sushinotokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Sujin}}. He is considered the distant ancestor of {{ill|Oomizuguchi no Sukune|ja|大水口宿禰|lt=Oomizuguchi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomizuguchi no Sukune|qq=}}. Furthermore, he traces his lineage back to {{ill|Utsushiko'o-no-Mikoto|ja|内色許男命|lt=Utsushiko'o-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushiko'o-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a sibling of {{ill|Utsushikome|lt=Utsushikome|lt=Utsushiko'o-no-Mikoto's sister|ja|鬱色謎命|lt=Utsushikome|lt=Utsushiko'o-no-Mikoto's sister|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushiko'o-no-Mikoto's sister|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 欝色謎命}}, who became the consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}} and gave birth to {{ill|Ohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Ohiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kōka|1=Emperor Kaika}}. Additionally, in the '''Kojiki''' (『古事記』), the ancestor of the Hozumi Clan, {{ill|Keninoyama Tarine|ja|建忍山垂根|lt=Keninoyama Tarine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keninoyama Tarine|qq=}}, is said to have a daughter, {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Oninokimi no Miko|ja|弟橘媛|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Oninokimi no Miko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oninokimi no Miko|qq=}}, who became the consort of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|日本武尊|lt=Emperor Jimmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}, and her father, {{ill|Hozumi Clan no Shuneyama Sukune|ja|穂積氏忍山宿禰|lt=Hozumi Clan no Shuneyama Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Clan no Shuneyama Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is considered the same person as {{ill|Keninoyama Tarine|ja|建忍山垂根|lt=Keninoyama Tarine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keninoyama Tarine|qq=}} in the '''Nihon Shoki''' (『日本書紀』). == Overview == The first recorded activities of the Hozumi Clan appear in the early 6th century with {{ill|Hozumi Oshiyama|ja|穂積押山|lt=Hozumi Oshiyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Oshiyama|qq=}}, who served {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}. In the 6th year of Emperor Keitai (circa 600), Oshiyama was appointed as a messenger to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and stationed in {{ill|Mimana|en|Mimana|qid=Q1790223|lt=Mimana|1=Mimana}}, where he became the provincial governor of {{ill|Darikoku|ja|哆唎の国守|lt=Darikoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Darikoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is credited with persuading the cession of four provinces to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. Despite his {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|qid=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}} surname, in the '''Baekje Honki''' (『百済本記』), he is referred to as "Ine Ikuyama Kimiya" (委意斯移麻岐彌), suggesting a special relationship with the Baekje king, similar to how {{ill|Kawachi-kuni no Mikoto|ja|河内国造|lt=Kawachi-kuni no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-kuni no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was granted the name "Kurahashi-kimi" for his military achievements in 554.{{Cite journal|language=ja|author=Lee Jae-seok|date=2004-03|title=Sixth Century Japanese-Baekje Officials and Their Nature|journal=Komazawa Historical Society|volume=62|publisher=Komazawa Historical Society|url=|page=|crid=1050564288184403072}} The next recorded member, {{ill|Hozumi Iwayumi|ja|穂積磐弓|lt=Hozumi Iwayumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Iwayumi|qq=}}, was appointed in the 16th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} (684) on July 4. Together with {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|qid=Q595897|lt=Soga Iname|1=Soga no Iname}}, he was dispatched to {{ill|Kibi Province|en|Kibi Province|qid=Q1155128|lt=Kibi Province|1=Kingdom of Kibi}} to establish the {{ill|Shirai no Miyake|ja|白猪屯倉|1=Shirai no Miyake|lt=Shirai no Miyake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Miyake|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Miyake|qq=}}. {{ill|Hozumi Soso|ja|穂積祖足|lt=Hozumi Soso|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Soso|qq=}} was appointed as the {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=}} in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|lt=Emperor Shōmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} (672). He led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to assist {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|lt=Mimana Nihonfu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6139814}} in the Jinshin War (壬申の乱) against {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Shinra|1=Silla}}, capturing five castles and subduing Shinra. In the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}, {{ill|Hozumi no Kujii|lt=Hozumi no Kujii|lt=Hozumi So|ja|穂積咋|lt=Hozumi no Kujii|lt=Hozumi So|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi So|qq=}} became {{ill|Koetsuga|ja|小乙下|lt=Koetsuga|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koetsuga|qq=}} and {{ill|Daisenjō|ja|大山上|lt=Daisenjō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenjō|qq=}}, appointed as the first {{ill|Governor of the Eastern Provinces|ja|東国国司|lt=Governor of the Eastern Provinces|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of the Eastern Provinces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the first year of the {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|qid=Q1077930|lt=Taika era|1=Taika (era)}} (645). In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kotoku|1=Emperor Kōgen}} (649), he surrounded the {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|en|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|qid=Q3488899|lt=Tadaka Soga Yamada Iwami Maro|1=Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro}}, the {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|qid=Q1351174|lt=Right Minister|1=Minister of the Right}}, suspected of rebellion, at {{ill|Yamada-dera|en|Yamada-dera|qid=Q3571406|lt=Yamada-dera|1=Yamada-dera}}. After enclosing the area with troops, he decapitated Yamada Iwami Maro, who had already committed suicide. {{ill|Hozumi no Momotari|lt=Hozumi no Momotari|lt=Hozumi Momotatsu|ja|穂積百足|lt=Hozumi no Momotari|lt=Hozumi Momotatsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Momotatsu|qq=}}, his son, along with his brothers {{ill|Hozumi no Ioe|lt=Hozumi no Ioe|lt=Hozumi Gohyakueda|ja|穂積五百枝|lt=Hozumi no Ioe|lt=Hozumi Gohyakueda|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Gohyakueda|qq=}}, initially acted as envoys to mobilize troops for {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大海人皇子|lt=Otomo no Otomaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Otomaro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇}} (Emperor Kōbun), but after his brother Momotatsu was killed and lost command, he sided with {{ill|Prince Yamabe|ja|大海人皇子|lt=Prince Yamabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamabe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} (Emperor Tenmu). In the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|lt=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} (684), with the establishment of the {{ill|Eight Kabane system|ja|八色の姓|lt=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachishiro no Kabane|qq=}}, the Hozumi Clan was granted one of the 52 surnames. {{ill|Naokiro Naoha|ja|直広肆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naokiro Naoha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hozumi Mushimaro|ja|穂積虫麻呂|lt=Hozumi Mushimaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Mushimaro|qq=}} were assigned as {{ill|Shuchō|en|Shuchō|qid=Q777413|lt=Washi|1=Shuchō}} in the first year of {{ill|Shucho era|ja|朱鳥元年|lt=Shucho era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shucho era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to entertain {{ill|Kinchi|ja|金智祥|lt=Kinchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as envoys to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] along with {{ill|Kawachi-no-Mikoto|ja|川内王|lt=Kawachi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内王}} and {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|lt=Otomo no Otomaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Otomaro|qq=}}. In the 3rd year of {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|qid=Q1373026|lt=Wado era|1=Wadō (era)}} (710), {{ill|Hozumi no Kako|ja|穂積賀枯|lt=Hozumi no Kako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Kako|qq=}} was appointed as {{ill|Sanni Ryo|ja|散位寮|lt=Sanni Ryo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanni Ryo|qq=}} as a successor to {{ill|Kudara no Konikishi Rizen|ja|百済王利善|lt=Kudara no Konikishi Rizen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudara no Konikishi Rizen|qq=}} in the 3rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku era|1=Enryaku}} (784), marking the end of official appointments for the Hozumi Clan and their disappearance from the central aristocracy. However, descendants from {{ill|Hozumi Nonimaro|ja|穂積濃美麻呂|lt=Hozumi Nonimaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi Nonimaro|qq=}} of the Kishu Kumano line Hozumi Clan, such as the {{ill|Fujishiro Suzuki Clan|ja|藤白鈴木氏|lt=Fujishiro Suzuki Clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Suzuki Clan|1=Fujishiro Suzuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18224450}}, emerged later, flourishing alongside the spread of Kumano faith. Additionally, {{ill|Hozumi Nobushige|en|Hozumi Nobushige|qid=Q799787|lt=Hozumi Ichiatsu|1=Hozumi Nobushige}}, a member of the Hozumi Clan from the Iyo Province's former Uwa District samurai family, is believed to be a progenitor of the drafters of the Japanese Civil Code.{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Osamu Shinmura|title=『日本古代氏族人名辞典』|date=1990th year |publisher=吉川弘文館|isbn=978-4-642-02243-0}}{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Suzuki Satoshi|title=『穂積姓鈴木氏 : 紀州徳川二七〇年鈴木家系譜』|publisher=私家版|year=1982|isbn=}}{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Takashi Hozumi|title=『古代氏族系譜集成 中巻』|publisher=古代氏族研究会|year=1986|isbn=|url=}}{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Yoshimura Takehiko|title=『国史大辞典』|date=|publisher=吉川弘文館|isbn=|volume=2}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『古事記注解』|date=1993rd year 6月|publisher=笠間書院|author=Shinno Shitaramitsu & Yamaguchi Yoshinori|isbn=978-4-305-60010-3|volume=2}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『穂積姓鈴木氏 : 紀州徳川二七〇年鈴木家系譜』|publisher=私家版|year=1982|isbn=|url=|page=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『古代氏族系譜集成 中巻』|publisher=古代氏族研究会|year=1986|isbn=|url=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『国史大辞典』|date=|publisher=吉川弘文館|author=Yoshimura Takehiko|isbn=|volume=2}} == External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/toyokumononokami/ Toyokumono-no-Mikoto – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] {{Japanese mythology (long)}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|穂積氏|version=100476592|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:穂積氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11597079}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
